compiled_state.title: #2 Step 0: extend parity tests — env-precedence matrix + remote git-fetch-semantics test
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description: Extend tests/interfaces/test_reducer_single_source.py + test_parity.py with the edge cases that gate every later step: empty store; archived on/off; alias/short/canonical id input; error/fsck_needed states; blocker permutations (closed/open/tombstoned-missing). Add an ENV-PRECEDENCE MATRIX (TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR-only / REBAR_ROOT-only / PROJECT_ROOT-only / cwd-only) asserting in-process == subprocess per read — this is the real Step-2 trap. Add a REMOTE-BACKED test pinning the chosen read-path git-fetch semantics (see Step 2 decision). AC: all assert at object level; suite green; serves as the merge gate.

comments.body: Done: tests/interfaces/test_native_read_parity.py (26 cases: archived on/off, alias/short/canonical, missing & archived targets, blocker permutations, every filter, empty store). Commit 7d53bc5.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
